concerns.text: T029 mandates halting execution if Marginal R² gain < 0.05. This violates SC-002 which defines this as a measured success criterion, not a hard gate. Research pipelines must report negative results (effect size < threshold) rather than aborting, preventing the generation of valid null findings.

T021 instructs loading the 'ECR-RS keyword proxy' while FR-004 and User Story 2 explicitly mandate the 'ECAR Lexicon'. This is a semantic weakening of the required instrument, risking invalid attachment proxy extraction without traceable justification.

T012.5 calculates the survey window from the *ingested* loneliness dataset but does not verify alignment with the *matched* user set. FR-002 requires logs to cover the window defined by the 'earliest and latest survey timestamps in the matched user set'. Filtering logs before matching may exclude valid data or include irrelevant logs.

T025.5 defines random effect structure in a config file. While this satisfies Constitution Principle VI (Pre-specification), the task description lacks the explicit 'pre-specified before data inspection' constraint found in the Constitution, creating a risk of post-hoc modification if not strictly enforced in the implementation logic.
